bird.name: Bolivian antpitta
bird.summary: The Bolivian antpitta (Grallaria cochabambae) is a bird in the family Grallariidae. The species was first described by James Bond and Rodol…

relations.subject: Bolivian antpitta
relations.evidence: It is found at elevations of 2,950–3,500 m. Its preferred habitat is humid montane forest and it prefers the understory and forest floor. The Bolivian antpitta is protected in some parts of its range including in Amboró National Park, Bolivia.{{Cite journal|last1=Chesser|first1=R
relations.object_summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。
